methods.description: This method describes the setup and execution of climate model simulations using the CCSM3 model to study climate trends over the last 9000 years. The model was initialized from a pre-industrial equilibrium state and integrated with orbital forcing to simulate Holocene climate changes.

steps.description: From a pre-industrial equilibrium simulation, the model was integrated for 400 yr with conditions representing 9 kyr BP orbital forcing to reach a new quasi-equilibrium.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate climate model for 400 years with 9 kyr BP orbital forcing

steps.description: After this spin-up, transient experiments were carried out by applying an acceleration (by a factor of 10) to the orbital forcing year until present-day.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct transient experiments with accelerated orbital forcing

steps.description: Conduct three Holocene transient experiments with different initial conditions within the available computer resources.
steps.searchable_summary: run three Holocene transient experiments with varied initial conditions

steps.description: Throughout the Holocene experiments, greenhouse gas concentrations as well as aerosol and ozone distributions were kept at pre-industrial values.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain pre-industrial greenhouse gas and aerosol levels in simulations

methods.description: This method involves analyzing results from multiple Holocene transient climate model simulations to study the evolution of the Southern Westerly Winds (SWW) under insolation changes. The models used include ECHO-G, COS-MOS, ECBilt-CLIO-VECODE, and CLIMBER2-LPJ.

steps.description: Analyze results from five other Holocene transient climate model simulations to study the evolution of the SWW under insolation changes.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze multiple climate model simulations for SWW evolution
inputs: Holocene transient climate model simulation results

steps.description: As in the CCSM3 transient runs, all these models have been forced by orbital variations only, keeping greenhouse gas concentrations constant at their pre-industrial levels.
steps.searchable_summary: process model data with constant pre-industrial greenhouse gas levels
